title: Repoint doctor's corrupt-recreate path at the canonical default_config_json
action: Delete doctor's local default_config_json (checks.rs:902-920) and its dedicated unit test default_config_json_seeds_default_agent_presets (checks.rs, tests mod). Repoint the corrupt-recreate call site (checks.rs:562, inside offer_config_fix) to call crate::config::default_config_json(&crate::config::default_config_root(home)) directly (per D2/D3, reusing cell -1's helper -- use crate::, never herdr_go::, this file is inside the lib crate). Note today's coverage gap: no existing test exercises offer_config_fix's actual unparseable-JSON branch (checks.rs:555-570) end-to-end -- only the now-deleted local function had a unit test, and only in isolation. Add a NEW test named exactly config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config in checks.rs's tests module (mirroring the style of config_fix_creates_missing_config / config_fix_repairs_empty_allowed_roots_via_added_root) that drives offer_config_fix with an unparseable config.json and asserts the recreated file contains agent_presets, so deleting the local function is a net gain in coverage of the real call site, not a loss. Verify greps for this exact function name, so name it precisely.

truths: offer_config_fix's corrupt-recreate path still writes a config.json containing agent_presets after this change, now proven by a test that exercises that exact branch

artifacts.substantive: no local default_config_json function remains; offer_config_fix's corrupt-recreate branch calls crate::config::default_config_json; a new test exercises the unparseable-JSON branch

key_links: checks.rs:562's call site references crate::config::default_config_json and crate::config::default_config_root
prohibitions: checks.rs:535's missing-file path (already calling config::ensure_config) is untouched
No net loss of test coverage for the corrupt-recreate path

verify: ! grep -q 'fn default_config_json' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'config::default_config_json' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'config::default_config_root' src/doctor/checks.rs && ! grep -q 'herdr_go::' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'fn config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config' src/doctor/checks.rs && cargo test --quiet config_fix_creates_missing_config && cargo test --quiet config_fix_repairs_empty_allowed_roots_via_added_root && cargo test --quiet config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config

trace.outcome: Deleted doctor's local default_config_json (checks.rs:898-920) and its isolated unit test; repointed offer_config_fix's corrupt-recreate call site (checks.rs:562) at crate::config::default_config_json(&crate::config::default_config_root(home)); added config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config, a new test exercising the real unparseable-JSON branch end-to-end (net gain in coverage vs the deleted isolated test).
trace.verify_output: All grep assertions passed (no local default_config_json fn, call site uses config::default_config_json + config::default_config_root, no herdr_go:: refs, new test fn present). All 3 cargo test filters: 1 test passed each (config_fix_creates_missing_config, config_fix_repairs_empty_allowed_roots_via_added_root, config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config), 0 failed. Exit 0.
trace.verify_command: ! grep -q 'fn default_config_json' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'config::default_config_json' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'config::default_config_root' src/doctor/checks.rs && ! grep -q 'herdr_go::' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'fn config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config' src/doctor/checks.rs && cargo test --quiet config_fix_creates_missing_config && cargo test --quiet config_fix_repairs_empty_allowed_roots_via_added_root && cargo test --quiet config_fix_recreates_unparseable_config
